Defines limits for JavaScript parser components like identifier length, string length, and code size.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines various limits for a JavaScript parser. It sets maximum values for different parser parameters, such as `PARSER_MAXIMUM_IDENT_LENGTH`, `PARSER_MAXIMUM_STRING_LENGTH`, `PARSER_MAXIMUM_NUMBER_OF_LITERALS`, `PARSER_MAXIMUM_NUMBER_OF_REGISTERS`, `PARSER_MAXIMUM_CODE_SIZE`, and `PARSER_MAXIMUM_STACK_LIMIT`. These limits ensure that the parser operates within defined constraints, preventing excessive resource usage or potential overflows. The file also includes preprocessor checks to validate that these limits are within acceptable ranges, and it generates compile-time errors if any limits are out of range. This helps maintain the stability and reliability of the parser by enforcing these constraints during the compilation process.
